msContents.summary: MISSING SINCE 1989. Liber amicorum. Containing 34 signed entries, including a few original drawings, by contemporary scholars, English and foreign. The entries are as under: 1. Sbastyn Sbasty (Johannes de). 22 March, 1619. 2. Sainte Catherine (A.). Heidelberg, 16/26 March, 1619. 3. Caus (Salomon de) [1576?-1630]. With emblazoned coat of arms, 16 March, 1619. 4. Fouquières (Jacques) [1580-1659]. Water-colour landscape, signed. n.d. 5. Sancourt-Villarnon (Zacarie de). 17 January, 1620. 6. Gruter (Janus) [1560-1627]. Heidelberg, 12 October, 1619. 7. Gothofred (Denis) [1549-1622]. Heidelberg, 17 October, 1619. 8. Delaune [Launeus] (Paul) [1583?-1654], M.D. London, 24 May, 1620. 9. Marius (Nathaniel). Partly in Hebrew. London, 17 December, 1619. 10. Asheworth (Henry), [M.D. Oxon.]. n.d. 11. Gillingham (George) [ -1668]. [Oxford], n.d. 12. Dickinson (William) [1585- ]. Oxford, 16 June, 1620. 13. Lingelsheim (George Michael). Heidelberg, 17 October, 1619. 14. Hawley (Richard) [1593-1636]. Oxford, 18 June, 1620. 15. Lapworth (Edward) [1574-1636], M.D. Oxford, 16 July, 1620. 16. Brosterhuisen (Johannes). London, 23 February, 1626. 17. Jungnitz (Christophorus). Heidelberg, 29 March, 1619. 18. Kimedoncius (Abraham). Heidelberg, 23 March [1619]. 19. Clayton [Clatton] (Thomas) [1575-1647]. Reg. Prof. M.D. Oxford, 26 June, 1620. 20. Burton (William) [1560-1623]. Med. Prof., Cambridge. n.d. 21. Gostlin (John) [1568?-1626]. Cambridge, n.d. 22. Chaloner (Edward) [1590-1625], of All Souls, Oxford, n.d. 23. Wheare (Degory) [1573-1647], of Oxford. n.d. 24. Cruso (Aquila) [1595-1660], of Cambridge. London, 29 September, 1621. 25. Gardiner (Richard) [1591-1670], of Oxford. n.p., 14 July, 1620. 26. Mel (J.). With emblematic pen-drawing. Heidelberg, 18 October, 1619. 27. Hoffbilthau (Leonhart von). With pen-drawing of nude man. Heidelberg, 24 October, 1619. 28. Cnopf (Raphael), of Danzig. London, 16 October, 1621. 29. Heinn (Samuel). Heidelberg, 20 March, 1619. 30. Chapman (Alexander) [1577-1629]. n.p., 19 March, 1619. 31. Rumpler (Jacob). Heidelberg, 20 March, 1619. 32. Schlieff (Christophorus), of Danzig. Heidelberg, 22 March, 1619. 33. Odryus (G.), of Geneva. n.p., n.d. 34. Pygott (Bartholomew). n.p., 18 October, 1619.

msItem.title: Chamberlayne, Peter (1601-1683)
msItem.filiation: Peter Chamberlayne, the original owner of this Album, was the third physician of his name, and was educated at Merchant Taylors School and Emmanuel College, Cambridge. He took his M.D. Degree at Padua in 1619, and was afterwards incorporated both at Oxford and Cambridge. [see the [i]Dictionary of National Biography[/i]].

objectDesc.supportDesc: 1 volume76 ll. (of which 46 are blank). 8vo. 151/2 x 91/2 cm. Contemporary gilt stamped morocco binding, with repeated floreated ornament and initials 'P.C.' in the centre, slightly rubbed.

binding.p: 76 ll. (of which 46 are blank). 8vo. 151/2 x 91/2 cm. Contemporary gilt stamped morocco binding, with repeated floreated ornament and initials 'P.C.' in the centre, slightly rubbed.

bibl.title: Catalogue of Western Manuscripts on Medicine and Science in the Wellcome Historical Medical Library
bibl.author: S. A. J. Moorat
bibl.publisher: Wellcome Institute for the History of Medicine

custodialHist.custEvent: Database description transcribed from S.A.J. Moorat, [i]Catalogue of Western Manuscripts on Medicine and Science in the Wellcome Historical Medical Library[/i] (London: Wellcome Institute for the History of Medicine, 1962-1973).

revisionDesc.change: Transformed Calm xml export to TEI xml file using a transformation scenario
Manual edits following automated Calm-TEI transformation
